mesa 0.99.1

Mutate columns in a qseaSet sample table

Source: R/dplyrVerbs.R
mutate.qseaSet.Rd

Extends dplyr::mutate() to add or modify columns in a qseaSet's sampleTable.

# S3 method for class 'qseaSet'
mutate(.data, ...)

Arguments

.data

qseaSet A qseaSet object whose sampleTable will be mutated.

...

Column transformations passed to dplyr::mutate(). For example, over70 = age > 70, group = paste(tissue, tumour, sep = "_"). Default: none.

Value

A qseaSet object with its sampleTable updated:

  • new columns are added,

  • existing columns are modified,

  • sample_name remains unchanged.

Details

The order of samples is preserved. The sample_name column cannot be changed with this function; use renameQsetNames() or renameSamples() if sample names must be updated.

See also

qsea::getSampleTable() to inspect metadata, renameSamples() and renameQsetNames() for renaming, filter.qseaSet() and selectQset() for other dplyr verbs.

Examples

data(exampleTumourNormal, package = "mesa")

# Add a new logical column based on age
exampleTumourNormal %>%
  mutate(over70 = age > 70) %>%
  qsea::getSampleTable()
#>          sample_name    group patient        type tumour tissue age gender
#> Colon1_N    Colon1_N Colon1_N  Colon1 NormalColon Normal  Colon  80      F
#> Colon1_T    Colon1_T Colon1_T  Colon1         CRC Tumour  Colon  80      F
#> Colon2_N    Colon2_N Colon2_N  Colon2 NormalColon Normal  Colon  67      F
#> Colon2_T    Colon2_T Colon2_T  Colon2         CRC Tumour  Colon  67      F
#> Lung1_N      Lung1_N  Lung1_N   Lung1  NormalLung Normal   Lung  64      F
#> Lung1_T      Lung1_T  Lung1_T   Lung1        LUAD Tumour   Lung  64      F
#> Lung2_N      Lung2_N  Lung2_N   Lung2  NormalLung Normal   Lung  71      F
#> Lung2_T      Lung2_T  Lung2_T   Lung2        LUSC Tumour   Lung  71      F
#> Lung3_N      Lung3_N  Lung3_N   Lung3  NormalLung Normal   Lung  72      M
#> Lung3_T      Lung3_T  Lung3_T   Lung3        LUAD Tumour   Lung  72      M
#>          stage over70
#> Colon1_N  <NA>   TRUE
#> Colon1_T   III   TRUE
#> Colon2_N  <NA>  FALSE
#> Colon2_T    IV  FALSE
#> Lung1_N   <NA>  FALSE
#> Lung1_T    IIA  FALSE
#> Lung2_N   <NA>   TRUE
#> Lung2_T     IA   TRUE
#> Lung3_N   <NA>   TRUE
#> Lung3_T     IB   TRUE

# Recode gender (better practice: use dplyr::case_when or stringr::str_replace)
exampleTumourNormal %>%
  mutate(gender = ifelse(gender == "F", "Female", "Male")) %>%
  qsea::getSampleTable()
#>          sample_name    group patient        type tumour tissue age gender
#> Colon1_N    Colon1_N Colon1_N  Colon1 NormalColon Normal  Colon  80 Female
#> Colon1_T    Colon1_T Colon1_T  Colon1         CRC Tumour  Colon  80 Female
#> Colon2_N    Colon2_N Colon2_N  Colon2 NormalColon Normal  Colon  67 Female
#> Colon2_T    Colon2_T Colon2_T  Colon2         CRC Tumour  Colon  67 Female
#> Lung1_N      Lung1_N  Lung1_N   Lung1  NormalLung Normal   Lung  64 Female
#> Lung1_T      Lung1_T  Lung1_T   Lung1        LUAD Tumour   Lung  64 Female
#> Lung2_N      Lung2_N  Lung2_N   Lung2  NormalLung Normal   Lung  71 Female
#> Lung2_T      Lung2_T  Lung2_T   Lung2        LUSC Tumour   Lung  71 Female
#> Lung3_N      Lung3_N  Lung3_N   Lung3  NormalLung Normal   Lung  72   Male
#> Lung3_T      Lung3_T  Lung3_T   Lung3        LUAD Tumour   Lung  72   Male
#>          stage
#> Colon1_N  <NA>
#> Colon1_T   III
#> Colon2_N  <NA>
#> Colon2_T    IV
#> Lung1_N   <NA>
#> Lung1_T    IIA
#> Lung2_N   <NA>
#> Lung2_T     IA
#> Lung3_N   <NA>
#> Lung3_T     IB

# Change group by removing digits from sample_name
exampleTumourNormal %>%
  mutate(group = stringr::str_remove(sample_name, "[0-9]")) %>%
  qsea::getSampleTable()
#>          sample_name   group patient        type tumour tissue age gender stage
#> Colon1_N    Colon1_N Colon_N  Colon1 NormalColon Normal  Colon  80      F  <NA>
#> Colon1_T    Colon1_T Colon_T  Colon1         CRC Tumour  Colon  80      F   III
#> Colon2_N    Colon2_N Colon_N  Colon2 NormalColon Normal  Colon  67      F  <NA>
#> Colon2_T    Colon2_T Colon_T  Colon2         CRC Tumour  Colon  67      F    IV
#> Lung1_N      Lung1_N  Lung_N   Lung1  NormalLung Normal   Lung  64      F  <NA>
#> Lung1_T      Lung1_T  Lung_T   Lung1        LUAD Tumour   Lung  64      F   IIA
#> Lung2_N      Lung2_N  Lung_N   Lung2  NormalLung Normal   Lung  71      F  <NA>
#> Lung2_T      Lung2_T  Lung_T   Lung2        LUSC Tumour   Lung  71      F    IA
#> Lung3_N      Lung3_N  Lung_N   Lung3  NormalLung Normal   Lung  72      M  <NA>
#> Lung3_T      Lung3_T  Lung_T   Lung3        LUAD Tumour   Lung  72      M    IB
